# Performance Metrics {#sec-performance-metrics}

```{r}
#| include: false
library(MSEtool)
library(ggplot2)
rnorm(5)
OM <- readRDS("../objects/OM/Ref.om")
FirstManagementYear <- 2028
```

Resolution 24-09 [@ICCAT2024Res2409] describes the initial operational management
objectives for Southern Atlantic Albacore, organised into four categories: *Stock Status*,
*Safety*, *Yield*, and *Stability*. 

Each performance metric (PM) below is calculated over the full 30-year projection period
by default, for a given CMP, and can be summarised at two levels: for an individual OM, or
across a set of OMs (e.g. the Reference Set). 

In the latter case, the simulations from every OM in the set are first combined into a single pooled
MSE object and the summary statistic (e.g. a probability or a mean) is then calculated
once over that combined set of simulations. This allows CMP performance to be summarised
both by individual uncertainty axis (e.g. by growth or natural mortality level) and
overall, across the full Reference OM set.

**Stock Status.** The stock should have a 60% or greater probability of occurring in the
green quadrant of the Kobe matrix over a 30-year projection period: i.e. $SB > SB_{MSY}$
and $F < F_{MSY}$ simultaneously.

**Safety.** There should be no greater than 15% probability of the stock falling below
$B_{LIM}$ at any point during the 30-year projection period, using an interim $B_{LIM}$ of
40% of $SB_{MSY}$ (footnote 1 of the Resolution). So
that higher values are better for every PM, this is expressed as the probability of the
stock remaining at or above $B_{LIM}$ throughout the projection period, with a target of
at least 85%.

**Yield.** The objective is to maximise overall catch levels. As the Resolution does not
specify a particular statistic, the mean annual catch is used. 

**Stability.** Any change in total allowable catch (TAC) between management periods
should be 20% or less, noting that asymmetric stability limits (e.g. different thresholds
for TAC increases versus decreases) may also be evaluated. This is expressed as the
probability that the change in TAC between successive management cycles is less than
20%; i.e. calculated between the TAC values actually set every `r Interval(OM)` years.

@tbl-pm-summary summarises these objectives, the corresponding performance metric used to
evaluate CMP performance against each, and the associated target. T

::: {#tbl-pm-summary}
| Objective | Performance Metric | Formula | Target |
|---|---|---|---|
| Stock Status | Probability of occurring in the Kobe green quadrant | $\text{Prob}\left(SB/SB_{MSY} > 1 \text{ and } F/F_{MSY} < 1\right)$ | $\geq 60\%$ |
| Safety | Probability of remaining at or above $B_{LIM}$ (40% $SB_{MSY}$) throughout the projection | $\text{Prob}\left(\min_y(SB_y/SB_{MSY}) \geq 0.4\right)$ | $\geq 85\%$ |
| Yield | Mean annual catch | $\text{mean}_y(C_y)$ | Maximise (ranked; no fixed target) |
| Stability | Probability that the change in TAC between management periods is $< 20\%$ | $\text{Prob}\left(\lvert (TAC_c - TAC_{c-1})/TAC_{c-1}\rvert < 0.2\right)$ | Ranked (no fixed probability target) |

Summary of the Resolution 24-09 [@ICCAT2024Res2409] initial operational management
objectives and the corresponding performance metrics used to evaluate CMP performance.
:::

::: {.callout-note}
## Projection Period Used for PM Calculation
These PMs are currently calculated over all projection years, starting in `r FirstManagementYear`
when the CMPs are first used to set a TAC (@sec-forward-projections). The SALB MSE
Technical Team may wish to consider excluding some initial number of years from this
period, to allow time for a CMP's TAC advice to actually take effect on the population
dynamics before performance is assessed.
:::
